tool_name: Fatigue Life Estimation — S-N Curve, Miner's Rule Cumulative Damage & Safety Factor

inputs.name: Stress Concentration Factor (K_t)
inputs.note: K_t=1 smooth bar; ~2 shoulder fillet; ~3–4 hole/notch — from Peterson's or FEA

inputs.name: Weld Detail Fatigue Class (FAT / Δσ_C at 2×10⁶ cycles)

options.label: FAT 125 — butt weld, full penetration, RT
FAT 71 — cruciform joint, fillet weld toe

inputs.name: S-N Curve Slope Exponent (m)
inputs.note: m=3 for welded details (IIW); m=5 base metal; second slope m=5 above knee per ASME

inputs.name: Total Expected Load Cycles Over Design Life (N_applied)
Mean Stress (σ_mean)
inputs.note: Positive = tensile; Goodman correction applied if σ_mean > 0

formulas: delta_sigma_eff = stress_range_mpa * stress_concentration_Kt   // [MPa] Effective Stress Range with Stress Concentration Reference: IIW Recommendations Doc. XIII-2460-13 §5.2

delta_sigma_corr = (mean_stress_mpa > 0) ? delta_sigma_eff_mpa / (1 - mean_stress_mpa / material_UTS_mpa) : delta_sigma_eff_mpa   // [MPa] Goodman Mean Stress Correction (Modified) Reference: Modified Goodman criterion — Shigley's 10th Ed. §6-12; IIW §5.5

N_ref = 2e6; N_allow = N_ref * Math.pow(fatigue_class_mpa / delta_sigma_corr_mpa, sn_slope_m)   // [cycles] Allowable Fatigue Life (N_allow) from S-N Curve Reference: IIW / EN 13445-3 Annex B — S-N curve: N = N_ref × (FAT/Δσ)^m
D_miner = applied_cycles_N / N_allow_cycles   // [dimensionless] Miner's Cumulative Damage Ratio (D) Reference: Palmgren-Miner rule — D < 1.0 required; D < 0.5 recommended for welded joints per IIW
SF_actual = N_allow_cycles / applied_cycles_N   // [dimensionless] Calculated Safety Factor on Life Reference: Life-based safety factor; compare to SF_target

delta_sigma_perm = fatigue_class_mpa * Math.pow(2e6 / (applied_cycles_N * safety_factor_target), 1/sn_slope_m)   // [MPa] Permissible Stress Range for Target Life (Δσ_perm) Reference: Design stress range ensuring N_allow = N_applied × SF_target

V1.error_msg: Applied stress range > 3× FAT class: extremely short fatigue life predicted — verify stress inputs or select higher FAT class

V2.error_msg: Mean stress ≥ UTS: static fracture condition — Goodman correction invalid; reduce mean stress

V3.error_msg: Fatigue life insufficient: Miner damage D > 1.0 — redesign geometry, reduce loads, or improve weld class

smart_warnings.message: D > 0.5 on welded joint: IIW §9.5.3 recommends D_limit = 0.5 for welded structures without fracture mechanics backup
Safety factor below target: reduce applied cycles, increase section, improve weld quality (higher FAT), or add postweld treatment
K_t > 3.0: severe notch effect — consider redesign to smooth geometry; shot peening or HFMI treatment can increase FAT class by 1–2 steps
Very high cycle regime (>10⁸): below ASTM E466 endurance limit boundary — verify S-N curve includes infinite-life region
Mean stress >50% UTS: Goodman correction significant — consider residual stress relief (PWHT) to reduce effective mean stress
